title: Encoding issue - API calls return incorrect charset
excerpt:

posts.author: mcd (marc codere dussault)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I am a bit confused as we had a perfectly functionning API setup. We never specified the charset to be used and always got appropriate results. However,as of this week (02-17-2025), API calls on say, item names, improperly return accents (say “é” as “Ã©”). Our setup hasn’t changed at all in several months.

Does anyone have any insight on to how to resolve this ? I tried using the charset=utf-8 in the API call, but results are no better…

Thanks!

posts.author: bmack (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m running into this issue as well.

I can’t confirm when it started breaking as non-English characters are a bit rare in our workspace, but the last successful one I can see was Dec 31, 2024.

I ran into the encoding issue as of Feb 17, 2025, potentially earlier.

posts.author: mcd (marc codere dussault)
posts.cookedHtml:
It’s possible that it was happening earlier.

I only got notifications that it was happening as of the 17th.

posts.author: Billchenau (Bill)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @mcd

Bill here from monday.com

So that we can look into this further would you be able to please fill this form adding as much information as possible to it (such as account ID, board IDs, item IDs, timestamps, etc.) so that our team can take a look into it?

Cheers,
Bill

posts.author: mcd (marc codere dussault)
posts.cookedHtml: I have submitted the request as described above.

posts.author: mcd (marc codere dussault)
posts.cookedHtml: FYI, I have determined that the break occured between these hours (eastern standard)
